steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 2 gives the T-38 jet as an example of a sleek, aerodynamic vehicle (glides easily). Sentence 3 describes a boxy semitruck that encounters more wind resistance — the opposite aerodynamic outcome. The blank connects two opposing examples introduced under the same general principle.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content: A. "As a result," — signals cause-and-effect: something happened because of what came before. Fails: the semitruck's wind resistance is not caused by the T-38's aerodynamics; they are independent contrasting examples. B. "Specifically," — signals a narrowing to a more detailed instance of the same thing just said. Fails: the semitruck is not a more specific version of the T-38; it is the opposite case. C. "In conclusion," — signals a summary of what was just argued. Fails: the passage is not wrapping up; it is still building its case with a second example. D. "On the other hand," — signals a contrasting idea of equal weight. Correctly pairs the aerodynamic T-38 (easy gliding) against the non-aerodynamic semitruck (high resistance). Correct. That matches choice D — D is correct.
